title: ledum palustre var. latifolium
intro: Ledum palustre var. latifolium, also known as bog rosemary, is a variety of the bog rosemary plant, a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to northern Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling, fight infections, and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Ledum palustre var. latifolium include its ability to improve respiratory health, relieve stress and anxiety, and support the immune system. Ledum palustre var. latifolium can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies, such as salves and liniments, to treat a range of health conditions.
